(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary:
Started the day early with Max around Fremont—he was pulling toward the park like always, and I let him. Grabbed a decent oat milk latte at our spot before heading to the station. The heat was already building by 8 AM, and I knew it'd be a scorcher.

Station 41 felt heavy today. Morning drills went smooth enough, but the smoke haze from BC was noticeable even inside. Couple of calls came through mid-afternoon—nothing major, just the usual summer stuff. Grabbed Molly Moon's for lunch because hydration, obviously. The crew was talking about the concert at Gas Works tonight; half of them wanted to catch it after shift, but I was already spent.

Dinner was quiet. We watched the heat shimmer off the parking lot and talked about the wildfire season ramping up. By the time 11 PM rolled around, I was ready to be home. Max was happy to see me.
thought_process: The heat and smoke made me hyper-aware of fire season arriving early—it's always in the back of my mind, but today felt like a warning. I skipped the concert idea because I knew I'd be useless; a 24-hour shift in 91-degree weather drains you different than a regular day. The crew seemed restless, probably eager to shake off the shift and enjoy the nice evening, but I've learned that knowing my limits matters more than FOMO. The smoke from BC also shifted something in me—it's a reminder that fire doesn't respect borders or schedules. I stayed focused on routine, hydration, and being present with the team when it mattered.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
